codeReview.summary: The feature successfully added a machine-readable /diagnostics/runtime surface and covered healthy/startup/degraded cases, but its initial readiness computation was incorrect for recovered runtimes that still had stale failure flags set. That made the diagnostics contract drift from actual recovered hot-endpoint behavior until the later recovery-state-alignment fix.

issues.description: `readiness.state` returns `ready` whenever `initialSyncCompleted` is true, even if `allowStaleLiveService` or `syncFailureReason` still indicate degraded fallback. This makes `/diagnostics/runtime` report a recovered/healthy state while stale-live fallback and provider-failure indicators remain active, violating the machine-readable readiness/degraded contract and the feature's expected behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that the shared HTTP app test fixture auto-completes bootstrap before `inject`, so route tests cannot represent a true pre-ready startup state. That constraint is not recorded in mission shared state even though it affected how runtime-health features had to be tested.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 8d269873-0f29-42dd-9a78-aa3ff4f15965 notes that `buildApp(..., startBackgroundJobs: false)` still completes bootstrap before `inject`, forcing startup/degraded coverage into `tests/runtime-diagnostics.test.ts` instead of route-level probes.

summary: Fail: commit 04adc61 added the runtime diagnostics surface, but its readiness computation could report `ready` while degraded fallback indicators were still active. A later fix feature (`runtime-recovery-state-alignment-fix`) explicitly references and repairs this contract bug.
